questions.question: Explain the difference between Type 1 and Type 2 hypervisors with examples.
expectedPoints: Type 2 runs on a host operating system
Example of Type 1: VMware ESXi, Hyper-V
Example of Type 2: VirtualBox, VMware Workstation
Type 1 generally offers better performance and security

questions.question: Discuss the various approaches to memory virtualization and their advantages.
expectedPoints: Shadow page tables: software-based mapping between guest and host memory
Extended Page Tables (EPT): hardware-assisted memory virtualization
Memory overcommitment: allocating more virtual memory than physically available
Memory ballooning: dynamically reclaiming memory from idle VMs
Advantages: better utilization, isolation, flexibility in memory management
